title: Bridge Essay: Human self and Neurosurgery: Advances and insights from Geneva.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Human, self, and, Neurosurgery:, Advances.

**Human self and Neurosurgery: Advances and insights from Geneva.** — Brain Spine. PMID: 40895030. DOI: 10.1016/j.bas.2025.104385. The preservation of the human self-afundamental yet underexplored aspect of neurosurgical practice-has gained increasing attention in recent years. How can neural correlates of self-consciousness be identified, monitored, and protected during brain tumor surgery, and how might this reshape the concept of "onco-functional balance"? This review synthesizes emerging evidence from neuroimaging, neuropsychology, and intraoperative neurophysiology to build aframework for integrating the concept of s
